channel.title: Instrumentation on Nicole van der Hoeven
channel.description: Recent content in Instrumentation on Nicole van der Hoeven

item.title: Behind the Magic of Auto-Instrumentation (Grafana ❤️🔥 OpenTelemetry Community Call #6)
item.description: In this community call with my coworker Liudmila Molkova, we pulled back the curtain on OpenTelemetry’s auto-instrumentation capabilities. We explored how auto-instrumentation works under the hood, when to use it versus manual instrumentation, and the trade-offs involved. The discussion covered language-specific implementations, the Collector’s role in auto-instrumentation, and practical tips for getting started with zero-code observability.

item.title: How to do continuous profiling right with Grafana Pyroscope and Ryan Perry, (Grafana Office Hours #26)
item.description:
My friend Paul Balogh and I interview Ryan Perry, co-founder of Grafana Pyroscope, about how to do continuous profiling right. Ryan, and the rest of the Pyroscope team, joined Grafana Labs, where he is now a Director of Engineer. Ryan discusses the main concerns in continuous profiling and how to avoid those pitfalls. Pyroscope is an open-source project for aggregating continuous profiling data about your system’s resources.

Links

  • Pyroscope docs
  • Pyroscope site
  • Pyroscope repo
  • Weigh in on the OpenTelemetry Profiling Data Model

item.title: Introduction to eBPF With Grafana Beyla (Grafana Office Hours #25)
item.description:
Nikola Grčevski, Principal Software Engineer at Grafana Labs, gives us an introduction to eBPF with Grafana Beyla. We discuss what is eBPF, how you can use it to auto-instrument applications, and how to get started with Beyla. eBPF observability is all the rage because we all want automagical instrumentation– but does it live up to that promise? Nikola’s here to tell us what eBPF can and can’t do, and where he’d like to take Beyla next.

Links

  • Grafana Beyla repo
  • Grafana Beyla docs
  • Application Observability docs
